title.en: Season 2 Finale: "I felt so supported thanks to you."
title.ko: 덕분에 정말 든든했어요 (진심 어린 감사와 시즌 2 마무리)

description.en: It’s the grand finale! Learn how to express deep gratitude and trust with the heartfelt phrase "덕분에 정말 든든했어요" as we wrap up Season 2.
description.ko: 시즌 2의 마지막 에피소드! 그동안 함께해준 사람에게 깊은 감사를 전하고, 서로의 앞날을 응원하는 가장 따뜻한 피날레 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Friendship. Gratitude. Trust. Farewell. Season Finale. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I can’t believe it! We’ve finally reached our 30th episode of Season 2.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Looking back, I’ve learned so many "real" Korean reactions that I could never find in a textbook. I feel like a completely different person now.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 저도 존이 성장하는 모습을 보면서 정말 뿌듯했어요. 이제 웬만한 한국인보다 리액션을 더 잘하시던걸요?
content.translation: I was really proud to see you grow too, John. You react even better than most Koreans now!

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's all because of you, Mina. I want to tell you how much I appreciated having you by my side. In English, I'd say "I felt so safe with you," but is there a deeper Korean word for that?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "든든하다" 라는 단어가 딱이에요. "미나씨 덕분에 정말 든든했어요" 라고 하면 그 어떤 감사 인사보다 더 깊은 진심이 전달되거든요.
content.translation: In that case, the word "Deun-deun-hada" is perfect. If you say, "I felt so supported/reassured thanks to you, Mina," it conveys deeper sincerity than any other thank-you.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 시즌2를 아름답게 마무리하는 최고의 감사 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn the 'best gratitude reactions' to beautifully wrap up Season 2!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미나 씨 덕분에 정말 든든했어요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "든든하다"... This word feels like having a strong shield behind me.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 단순히 고마운 게 아니라 상대방이 내 곁에 있어서 마음이 놓이고 의지가 됐다는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: Right! It doesn't just mean you're thankful; it means you felt at ease and could lean on them because they were by your side.

insight.title: 덕분에 정말 든든했어요
insight.description: 'I felt so supported/reassured thanks to you'
insight.usage_tip: 덕분에(Thanks to/Because of) + 정말(Really) + 든든했어요(Felt supported/safe). Use this when someone has been a great help or emotional support to you.

content.speaker: John
content.text: And how do I say I've learned a lot from them?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "덕분에 많이 배웠습니다" 라고 정중하게 말해 보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying politely, "I learned a lot thanks to you."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 상대방을 스승처럼 존중하면서 감사를 표하는 아주 품격 있는 리액션이죠.
content.translation: It's a very classy reaction that expresses gratitude while respecting the other person like a teacher.

insight.title: 덕분에 많이 배웠습니다
insight.description: 'I learned a lot thanks to you'
insight.usage_tip: This is the standard polite phrase used at the end of a project, a class, or a long period of working together.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. It’s the last day of our Season 2 project. Mina and I are sharing our final thoughts.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 벌써 29화가 끝났네요. 그동안 정말 고생 많으셨어요.
content.translation: John, episode 29 is already over. You've really worked hard all this time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 제가 더 감사하죠. 미나 덕분에 정말 든든했어요.
content.translation: No, I'm more thankful. I felt so supported thanks to you, Mina.

insight.title: 미나 씨 덕분에
insight.usage_tip: Putting the person's name before '덕분에' makes the sentiment feel much more direct and warm.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그렇게 말씀해 주시니 저야말로 정말 든든하네요! 존 씨가 질문도 많이 해주셔서 저도 즐거웠어요.
content.translation: Since you said it that way, I feel really supported too! I had fun too because you asked so many questions, John.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부족한 저를 잘 이끌어 주셔서 덕분에 많이 배웠습니다. 정말 감사합니다!
content.translation: Thank you for leading me well despite my shortcomings. I learned a lot thanks to you. Thank you so much!

insight.title: 부족한 저를 잘 이끌어 주셔서
insight.description: 'Thank you for leading my lacking self'
insight.usage_tip: A humble way to acknowledge the other person's guidance.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니에요. 존은 이미 최고예요! 우리 시즌 3에서는 더 재미있는 리액션으로 만나요!
content.translation: No, John, you're already the best! Let's meet with even more fun reactions in Season 3!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, Season 2 really was an amazing journey. I feel like my Korean has "heart" now, not just words.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 진정한 리액션은 단어가 아니라 그 속에 담긴 진심에서 나오니까요.
content.translation: Right. True reactions come from the 'sincerity' inside, not the words.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "Season Finale" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 덕분에 정말 든든했어요!
content.translation: I felt so supported thanks to you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 덕분에 많이 배웠습니다!
content.translation: I learned a lot thanks to you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그동안 고생 많으셨어요!
content.translation: You’ve worked hard all this time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 좋은 추억 만들어 주셔서 감사해요!
content.translation: Thank you for making great memories!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 우리 꼭 다시 만나요!
content.translation: Let's definitely meet again!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분, 시즌 2의 29화까지 함께해주셔서 정말 감사합니다. 여러분이 있어서 저희도 정말 든든했어요!
content.translation: Everyone, thank you so much for being with us until the 29th episode of Season 2. Because of you, we also felt very supported!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "덕분에 정말 든든했어요!"
content.translation: I felt so supported thanks to you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: We’ll be back with even more "real" and "spicy" Korean reactions. Until then!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 친구가 내 편을 들어주니 정말 든든하네요. (I feel so supported since my friend is on my side.)
선생님 덕분에 시험에 합격할 수 있었어요. (I was able to pass the exam thanks to my teacher.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be proud / To feel a sense of accomplishment
vocabulary.example: 열심히 공부해서 상을 받으니 정말 뿌듯해요. (I'm so proud to receive an award after studying hard.)
리더는 팀원들을 올바른 방향으로 이끌어야 해요. (A leader should guide team members in the right direction.)
존 씨의 한국어 실력이 지난달보다 많이 성장했어요. (John's Korean skills have grown a lot since last month.)
멋진 피날레 공연으로 축제가 마무리되었어요. (The festival ended with a wonderful finale performance.)
